expectations.text: States core belt conclusions are largely unchanged and a full rewrite is not urgently needed
expectations.evidence: 'The Belt Bible's core conclusions hold up well for deadlifts. The mechanistic understanding remains sound, and the practical recommendations haven't fundamentally shifted.'

expectations.text: Mentions at least one mechanism: higher intra-abdominal pressure can increase hip extension torque
expectations.evidence: 'Elevated IAP does two critical things: 1. Stiffens the spine against compressive and shear forces. 2. Generates passive hip extension torque — the belt gives your trunk something to push against, allowing you to produce more force through the hips.'

expectations.text: Concludes belts still make sense for many lifters and old guidance is mostly intact
expectations.evidence: 'The practical recommendations from the Belt Bible remain solid.' Lists unchanged guidance: belts don't weaken core, use for heavy sets, train some beltless, belt fit matters.
